channel.title: The Pittsburgh Daily Herald
channel.description: Local news for Pittsburgh and the river boroughs, saved for offline reading.

item.title: Millvale council approves dugout and backstop repairs at the community park
item.description: The borough set aside funds for new dugout benches and a repaired backstop before the spring youth season. Volunteer coaches asked for the work last fall.

item.title: Cold snap expected by the weekend, first hard frost likely overnight
item.description: Gardeners are advised to bring in the last of the tomatoes and peppers. Lows near 28 are forecast for the North Hills and the river towns.

item.title: Steelers lean on the defense heading into a road test at Cincinnati
item.description: The black and gold ride a three game stretch of stingy defense into the division road game. The run game will decide whether they hold their spot in the standings.

item.title: St. Nicholas Parish in Millvale sets its Advent and St. Nicholas Day schedule
item.description: The parish announced confession times, the children's St. Nicholas Day blessing on December 6, and a giving tree for families in need. The famous murals will be lit for the season.

item.title: Retired firefighters host pancake breakfast to fund the relief association
item.description: Members of several former engine companies served breakfast downtown. Proceeds go to the widows and orphans relief fund and to scholarships for cadets.

item.title: Penguins rally in the third to take down a division rival at home
item.description: Two late goals turned a one goal deficit into a win at the arena. The power play, quiet for a week, finally broke through in the third period.

item.title: Millvale library extends weekday hours and adds a children's reading club
item.description: The branch will stay open until seven on weekdays. A new club for early readers meets on Saturday mornings, with a waiting list already forming.

item.title: Millvale borough posts final leaf pickup schedule before the freeze
item.description: Public works will run the last two curbside leaf collections the weeks of November 16 and November 23. Residents are asked to rake to the curb, not the street, and to skip the plastic bags.

item.title: County treasurer reminds homeowners that real estate installments are due
item.description: Property owners who pay directly rather than through escrow were reminded to send the county portion before the face deadline to avoid the penalty.

item.title: Pirates add a young arm and talk up the farm system at the winter meetings
item.description: The club signed a reliever and said the pitching depth in the minors is the best it has been in years. Fans have heard that before, but the arms are real this time.

item.title: Youth baseball registration for the spring season opens next month
item.description: League organizers across the river boroughs said sign ups open in December for children ages seven to ten. Volunteer coaches and umpires are needed.

item.title: River level advisory lifted as flows return to normal on the Allegheny
item.description: Officials lifted the advisory after a dry week. Anglers and boaters were told conditions along the lower pools should be steady through the holiday.
